---
title: "Routes Dashboard"
format: dashboard
---

```{python}
from pathlib import Path
import json
import pandas as pd

manifest = json.loads(Path("data/routes.json").read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
routes = pd.DataFrame(manifest["routes"])
summary_table = pd.DataFrame(
    [
        {
            "Route": route["title"],
            "Miles": route["summary"]["distance_mi"],
            "Gain (ft)": route["summary"]["elevation_gain_ft"],
            "Loss (ft)": route["summary"]["elevation_loss_ft"],
            "Max Elevation (ft)": route["summary"]["max_elevation_ft"],
            "Segments": route["summary"]["segment_count"],
        }
        for route in manifest["routes"]
    ]
)

hazard_rows = []
for route in manifest["routes"]:
    hazard_path = Path(route["paths"]["hazards"])
    hazards = pd.read_json(hazard_path)
    hazards = hazards[hazards["hazard"] != "TOTAL"].copy()
    hazards["Route"] = route["title"]
    hazard_rows.append(hazards[["Route", "hazard", "distance_mi", "percent"]])

hazards_table = pd.concat(hazard_rows, ignore_index=True)
hazards_table["hazard"] = hazards_table["hazard"].str.replace("_", " ").str.title()
```

## Snapshot

### Route Summaries

```{python}
summary_table
```

### Top Hazards

```{python}
hazards_table.sort_values(["Route", "distance_mi"], ascending=[True, False]).head(20)
```

## Notes

### Editorial Direction

- Use this page for cross-route comparison.
- Use the per-route notes for maps, route-specific observations, and narrative.
- Keep route analysis precomputed in Python and presentation authored in Quarto.
